children.text: developed by the State educational agency with timely and meaningful consultation with the Governor, members of the State legislature and State board of education (if the State has a State board of education), local educational agencies (including those located in rural areas), representatives of Indian tribes located in the State, teachers, principals, other school leaders, charter school leaders (if the State has charter schools), specialized instructional support personnel, paraprofessionals, administrators, other staff, and parents; and

children.label: B
children.text: is coordinated with other programs under this chapter, the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.), the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), the Carl D. children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Consultation required under paragraph (1)(A) shall not interfere with the timely submission of the plan required under this section.

children.heading: Consolidated plan
children.label: 3 Consolidated plan
children.text: A State plan submitted under paragraph (1) may be submitted as part of a consolidated plan under .section 7842 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: establish a peer-review process to assist in the review of State plans;

children.label: aa
children.text: parents, teachers, principals, other school leaders, specialized instructional support personnel, State educational agencies, local educational agencies, and the community (including the business community); and

children.label: AA
children.text: the implementation of academic standards, assessments, or accountability systems; and

children.label: BB
children.text: how to meet the needs of disadvantaged students, children with disabilities, and English learners, the needs of low-performing schools, and other educational needs of students;

children.label: II
children.text: that include, to the extent practicable, majority representation of individuals who, in the most recent 2 years, have had practical experience in the classroom, school administration, or State or local government (such as direct employees of a school, local educational agency, or State educational agency); and

children.label: III
children.text: who represent a regionally diverse cross-section of States;

children.label: iii
children.text: make available to the public, including by such means as posting to the Department’s website, the list of peer reviewers who have reviewed State plans under this section;

children.label: iv
children.text: ensure that the peer-review teams consist of varied individuals so that the same peer reviewers are not reviewing all of the State plans;

children.label: v
children.text: approve a State plan not later than 120 days after its submission, unless the Secretary meets the requirements of clause (vi);

children.label: aa
children.text: determines how the State plan fails to meet the requirements of this section;

children.label: bb
children.text: immediately provides to the State, in writing, notice of such determination, and the supporting information and rationale to substantiate such determination;

children.label: AA
children.text: technical assistance to assist the State in meeting the requirements of this section;

children.label: BB
children.text: in writing, all peer-review comments, suggestions, recommendations, or concerns relating to its State plan; and

children.label: CC
children.text: a hearing, unless the State declines the opportunity for such hearing; and

children.label: aa
children.text: does not revise and resubmit its State plan; or

children.label: bb
children.text: in a case in which a State revises and resubmits its State plan after a hearing is conducted under subclause (I)(cc)(CC), or after the State has declined the opportunity for such a hearing, the Secretary determines that such revised State plan does not meet the requirements of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: maximize collaboration with each State;

children.label: ii
children.text: promote effective implementation of the challenging State academic standards through State and local innovation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provide transparent, timely, and objective feedback to States designed to strengthen the technical and overall quality of the State plans.

children.heading: Standard and nature of review
children.label: C Standard and nature of review
children.text: Peer reviewers shall conduct an objective review of State plans in their totality and out of respect for State and local judgments, with the goal of supporting State- and local-led innovation and providing objective feedback on the technical and overall quality of a State plan.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: D Prohibition
children.text: Neither the Secretary nor the political appointees of the Department, may attempt to participate in, or influence, the peer-review process.

children.label: A
children.text: plans submitted or resubmitted by a State;

children.label: B
children.text: peer-review guidance, notes, and comments and the names of the peer reviewers (once the peer reviewers have completed their work);

children.label: C
children.text: State plan determinations by the Secretary, including approvals or disapprovals; and

children.label: D
children.text: notices and transcripts of hearings under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: remain in effect for the duration of the State’s participation under this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be periodically reviewed and revised as necessary by the State educational agency to reflect changes in the State’s strategies and programs under this part.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If a State makes significant changes to its plan at any time, such as the adoption of new challenging State academic standards or new academic assessments under subsection (b), or changes to its accountability system under subsection (c), such information shall be submitted to the Secretary in the form of revisions or amendments to the State plan.

children.heading: Review of revised plans
children.label: ii Review of revised plans
children.text: The Secretary shall review the information submitted under clause (i) and approve changes to the State plan, or disapprove such changes in accordance with paragraph (4)(A)(vi), within 90 days, without undertaking the peer-review process under such paragraph.

children.heading: Special rule for standards
children.label: iii Special rule for standards
children.text: If a State makes changes to its challenging State academic standards, the requirements of subsection (b)(1), including the requirement that such standards need not be submitted to the Secretary pursuant to subsection (b)(1)(A), shall still apply.

children.heading: Failure to meet requirements
children.label: 7 Failure to meet requirements
children.text: If a State fails to meet any of the requirements of this section, the Secretary may withhold funds for State administration under this part until the Secretary determines that the State has fulfilled those requirements.

children.heading: Public comment
children.label: 8 Public comment
children.text: Each State shall make the State plan publicly available for public comment for a period of not less than 30 days, by electronic means and in an easily accessible format, prior to submission to the Secretary for approval under this subsection. The State, in the plan it files under this subsection, shall provide an assurance that public comments were taken into account in the development of the State plan.

children.heading: Challenging academic standards and academic assessments
children.label: b Challenging academic standards and academic assessments

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State, in the plan it files under subsection (a), shall provide an assurance that the State has adopted challenging academic content standards and aligned academic achievement standards (referred to in this chapter as “challenging State academic standards”), which achievement standards shall include not less than 3 levels of achievement, that will be used by the State, its local educational agencies, and its schools to carry out this part. A State shall not be required to submit such challenging State academic standards to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: apply to all public schools and public school students in the State; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to academic achievement standards, include the same knowledge, skills, and levels of achievement expected of all public school students in the State.

children.heading: Subjects
children.label: C Subjects
children.text: The State shall have such academic standards for mathematics, reading or language arts, and science, and may have such standards for any other subject determined by the State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each State shall demonstrate that the challenging State academic standards are aligned with entrance requirements for credit-bearing coursework in the system of public higher education in the State and relevant State career and technical education standards.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: ii Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to authorize public institutions of higher education to determine the specific challenging State academic standards required under this paragraph.

children.heading: Alternate academic achievement standards for students with the most significant cognitive disabilities
children.label: E Alternate academic achievement standards for students with the most significant cognitive disabilities
I
children.text: are aligned with the challenging State academic content standards under subparagraph (A);

children.label: II
children.text: promote access to the general education curriculum, consistent with the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1400

children.label: III
children.text: reflect professional judgment as to the highest possible standards achievable by such students;

children.label: IV
children.text: are designated in the individualized education program developed under section 614(d)(3) of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act () for each such student as the academic achievement standards that will be used for the student; and20 U.S.C. 1414(d)(3)

children.label: V
children.text: are aligned to ensure that a student who meets the alternate academic achievement standards is on track to pursue postsecondary education or employment, consistent with the purposes of [ et seq.], as in effect on .Public Law 93–11229 U.S.C. 701July 22, 2014

children.heading: Prohibition on any other alternate or modified academic achievement standards
children.label: ii Prohibition on any other alternate or modified academic achievement standards
children.text: A State shall not develop, or implement for use under this part, any alternate academic achievement standards for children with disabilities that are not alternate academic achievement standards that meet the requirements of clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: are derived from the 4 recognized domains of speaking, listening, reading, and writing;

children.label: ii
children.text: address the different proficiency levels of English learners; and

children.label: iii
children.text: are aligned with the challenging State academic standards.

children.heading: Standards review or approval
children.label: i Standards review or approval
children.text: A State shall not be required to submit any standards developed under this subsection to the Secretary for review or approval.

children.heading: Federal control
children.label: ii Federal control
children.text: The Secretary shall not have the authority to mandate, direct, control, coerce, or exercise any direction or supervision over any of the challenging State academic standards adopted or implemented by a State.

children.heading: Existing standards
children.label: H Existing standards
children.text: Nothing in this part shall prohibit a State from revising, consistent with this section, any standards adopted under this part before or after .December 10, 2015

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each State plan shall demonstrate that the State educational agency, in consultation with local educational agencies, has implemented a set of high-quality student academic assessments in mathematics, reading or language arts, and science. The State retains the right to implement such assessments in any other subject chosen by the State.

children.label: I
children.text: the same academic assessments used to measure the achievement of all public elementary school and secondary school students in the State; and

children.label: II
children.text: administered to all public elementary school and secondary school students in the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: be aligned with the challenging State academic standards, and provide coherent and timely information about student attainment of such standards and whether the student is performing at the student’s grade level;

children.label: iii
children.text: be used for purposes for which such assessments are valid and reliable, consistent with relevant, nationally recognized professional and technical testing standards, objectively measure academic achievement, knowledge, and skills, and be tests that do not evaluate or assess personal or family beliefs and attitudes, or publicly disclose personally identifiable information;

children.label: iv
children.text: be of adequate technical quality for each purpose required under this chapter and consistent with the requirements of this section, the evidence of which shall be made public, including on the website of the State educational agency;

children.label: aa
children.text: in each of grades 3 through 8; and

children.label: bb
children.text: at least once in grades 9 through 12;

children.label: aa
children.text: grades 3 through 5;

children.label: bb
children.text: grades 6 through 9; and

children.label: cc
children.text: grades 10 through 12; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of any other subject chosen by the State, be administered at the discretion of the State;

children.label: vi
children.text: involve multiple up-to-date measures of student academic achievement, including measures that assess higher-order thinking skills and understanding, which may include measures of student academic growth and may be partially delivered in the form of portfolios, projects, or extended performance tasks;

children.label: I

children.text: The accountability provisions under this chapter shall be overseen for charter schools in accordance with State charter school law.

children.heading: In general

children.text: A local educational agency may provide all students enrolled in a school identified by the State for comprehensive support and improvement under subsection (c)(4)(D)(i) with the option to transfer to another public school served by the local educational agency, unless such an option is prohibited by State law.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: ii Priority
children.text: In providing students the option to transfer to another public school, the local educational agency shall give priority to the lowest-achieving children from low-income families, as determined by the local educational agency for the purposes of allocating funds to schools under .section 6313(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Treatment
children.label: iii Treatment
children.text: A student who uses the option to transfer to another public school shall be enrolled in classes and other activities in the public school to which the student transfers in the same manner as all other students at the public school.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: iv Special rule
children.text: A local educational agency shall permit a student who transfers to another public school under this paragraph to remain in that school until the student has completed the highest grade in that school.

children.heading: Funding for transportation
children.label: v Funding for transportation
children.text: A local educational agency may spend an amount equal to not more than 5 percent of its allocation under subpart 2 of this part to pay for the provision of transportation for students who transfer under this paragraph to the public schools to which the students transfer.

children.heading: Defining terms
children.label: 2 Defining terms
children.text: In carrying out this part, the Secretary shall not, through regulation or as a condition of approval of the State plan or revisions or amendments to the State plan, promulgate a definition of any term used in this part, or otherwise prescribe any specification for any such term, that is inconsistent with or outside the scope of this part or is in violation of paragraph (1).

children.label: f Existing State law

children.heading: Annual local educational agency report cards
children.label: 2 Annual local educational agency report cards

children.heading: Preparation and dissemination
children.label: A Preparation and dissemination
children.text: A local educational agency that receives assistance under this part shall prepare and disseminate an annual local educational agency report card that includes information on such agency as a whole and each school served by the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: concise;

children.label: ii
children.text: presented in an understandable and uniform format, and to the extent practicable, in a language that parents can understand; and

children.label: I
children.text: placing such report card on the website of the local educational agency; and

children.label: II
children.text: in any case in which a local educational agency does not operate a website, providing the information to the public in another manner determined by the local educational agency.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a local educational agency, information that shows how students served by the local educational agency achieved on the academic assessments described in subsection (b)(2) compared to students in the State as a whole;

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: any other information that the local educational agency determines is appropriate and will best provide parents, students, and other members of the public with information regarding the progress of each public school served by the local educational agency, whether or not such information is included in the annual State report card.

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: D Additional information
children.text: In the case of a local educational agency that issues a report card for all students, the local educational agency may include the information under this section as part of such report.

children.heading: Preexisting report cards
children.label: 3 Preexisting report cards
children.text: A State educational agency or local educational agency may use public report cards on the performance of students, schools, local educational agencies, or the State, that were in effect prior to , for the purpose of this subsection, so long as any such report card is modified, as may be needed, to contain the information required by this subsection, and protects the privacy of individual students.December 10, 2015

children.heading: Cost reduction
children.label: 4 Cost reduction
children.text: Each State educational agency and local educational agency receiving assistance under this part shall, wherever possible, take steps to reduce data collection costs and duplication of effort by obtaining the information required under this subsection through existing data collection efforts.

children.label: 1 In general

children.heading: Sufficiency
children.label: 2 Sufficiency
children.text: The reports described in subsection (h) shall only include data that are sufficient to yield statistically reliable information.

children.heading: Disaggregation
children.label: 3 Disaggregation
children.text: Disaggregation under this section shall not be required if such disaggregation will reveal personally identifiable information about any student, teacher, principal, or other school leader, or will provide data that are insufficient to yield statistically reliable information.

children.heading: Special rule with respect to Bureau-funded schools
children.label: k Special rule with respect to Bureau-funded schools

children.text: Each such school that is accredited by the State in which it is operating shall use the assessments and other academic indicators the State has developed and implemented to meet the requirements of this section, or such other appropriate assessment and academic indicators as approved by the Secretary of the Interior.

Each such school that is accredited by a regional accrediting organization (in consultation with and with the approval of the Secretary of the Interior, and consistent with assessments and academic indicators adopted by other schools in the same State or region) shall adopt an appropriate assessment and other academic indicators that meet the requirements of this section.

Each such school that is accredited by a tribal accrediting agency or tribal division of education shall use an assessment and other academic indicators developed by such agency or division, except that the Secretary of the Interior shall ensure that such assessment and academic indicators meet the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: l Construction
children.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed to prescribe the use of the academic assessments described in this part for student promotion or graduation purposes.
